codeReview.summary: The Gudang module is well implemented following the established Kas/Bank patterns. All core requirements are met: per-period database (/data/gudang/YYYY/MM.db), Gudang Masuk/Keluar CRUD, COA/Aspek Kerja/Blok linkage, item dropdown, filter/search, copyable tables, and dual approval workflow with 2 DIFFERENT approvers. The implementation correctly enforces: transactions start with 'Pending Approval 1' status, edit/delete only allowed for Pending transactions, unapproved transactions do NOT affect stock, and Fully Approved transactions affect stock calculations.

issues.description: Type mismatch: getGudangStatusOptions() returns string[] but should return TransactionStatus[] for consistency with the backend implementation. This is a minor type inconsistency but doesn't affect runtime behavior.

The canApprove function only checks if currentUser.role is 'Approver' or 'Administrator'. However, based on AGENTS.md, there's also an 'Inputan Gudang' role which may be intended for creation but not approval. This appears consistent with Kas/Bank patterns so likely intentional.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Gudang module follows the exact same implementation pattern as Kas and Bank modules. No deviations from established patterns were observed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/gudangDatabase.ts, src/main/kasDatabase.ts, src/main/bankDatabase.ts all use identical structure with same functions (createTransaction, updateTransaction, deleteTransaction, approveTransaction, rejectTransaction, etc.)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the worker skill procedure correctly. No deviations were noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true with no deviations.

summary: The Gudang module implementation successfully meets all requirements: per-period database storage, Gudang Masuk/Keluar CRUD operations, COA/Aspek Kerja/Blok linkage, item dropdown with name/unit, filter/search functionality, copyable tables with Ctrl+A+C support, Excel import/export, and dual approval workflow requiring 2 DIFFERENT approvers. The implementation correctly enforces all business rules: transactions start with 'Pending Approval 1', only Pending Approval 1 transactions can be edited/deleted, unapproved transactions do NOT affect stock, and only Fully Approved transactions affect stock calculations. No blocking issues were found.
